content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and MMM. You have to construct a matrix with NNN rows and MMM columns. Consider a multiset SSS which contains N+MN + MN+M integers: for each row and each column of this matrix, the MEX of the elements of this row/column belongs to SSS. Then, in the matrix you construct, the MEX of SSS must be maximum possible.

Note: The MEX of a multiset of integers is the smallest non-negative integer that is not present in this multiset. For example, MEX({4,9,0,1,1,5})=2\mathrm{MEX}(\{4,9,0,1,1,5\}) = 2MEX({4,9,0,1,1,5})=2 and MEX({1,2,3})=0\mathrm{MEX}(\{1,2,3\}) = 0MEX({1,2,3})=0.
constraints: the sum of N⋅MN \cdot MN⋅M over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

`Explanation**Example case 1:** The MEXs of the rows {9,1,0}\{9, 1, 0\}{9,1,0}, {1,0,2}\{1, 0, 2\}{1,0,2} and {7,5,8}\{7, 5, 8\}{7,5,8} are 222, 333 and 000 respectively. The MEXs of the columns {9,1,7}\{9, 1, 7\}{9,1,7}, {1,0,5}\{1, 0, 5\}{1,0,5} and {0,2,8}\{0, 2, 8\}{0,2,8} are 000, 222 and 111 respectively. Therefore, the multiset which contains the MEX of each row and each column is S={0,0,1,2,2,3}S = \{0, 0, 1, 2, 2, 3\}S={0,0,1,2,2,3} and MEX(S)=4\mathrm{MEX}(S) = 4MEX(S)=4. We can show that this is the largest MEX that can be obtained for a 3×33 \times 33×3 matrix.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print NNN lines. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines should contain MMM space-separated integers ― the elements in the iii-th row of your matrix. Each element of the matrix must be non-negative and must not exceed 10910^9109.

If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
